metadata.title: Framework for FDA's Real-World Evidence Program
metadata.document_version: Framework Document (per 21st Century Cures Act Section 505F)

rules.rule_text: Pursuant to 21st Century Cures Act Section 505F, FDA must evaluate the potential use of real-world evidence (RWE) to help support approval of new indications for drugs already approved under FD&C Act Section 505(c) or to help support or satisfy postapproval study requirements.
rules.source_section: Introduction, Scope of RWE Program Under 21st Century Cures Act
rules.verbatim_quote: Under the Cures Act, FDA's RWE Program must evaluate the potential use of RWD to generate RWE of product effectiveness to help support approval of new indications for drugs approved under FD&C Act Section 505(c) or to help support or satisfy postapproval study requirements. FDA's RWE Program will also apply to biological products licensed under section 351 of the Public Health Service Act.
rules.exceptions: The framework does not cover medical devices

rules.rule_text: Real-World Data (RWD) is defined as data relating to patient health status and/or the delivery of health care routinely collected from a variety of sources, and Real-World Evidence (RWE) is the clinical evidence about the usage and potential benefits or risks of a medical product derived from analysis of RWD.
rules.source_section: Introduction, Definitions of Real-World Data and Real-World Evidence
rules.verbatim_quote: Real-World Data (RWD) are data relating to patient health status and/or the delivery of health care routinely collected from a variety of sources. Real-World Evidence (RWE) is the clinical evidence about the usage and potential benefits or risks of a medical product derived from analysis of RWD.

rules.rule_text: FDA's three-part framework for evaluating RWE to support regulatory decisions requires assessment of: (1) whether the RWD are fit for use; (2) whether the trial or study design can provide adequate scientific evidence to answer the regulatory question; and (3) whether study conduct meets FDA regulatory requirements.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE for Use in Regulatory Decisions, Using Trials or Studies with RWD/RWE for Effectiveness Decisions
rules.verbatim_quote: The framework will include consideration of the following: 1. Whether the RWD are fit for use 2. Whether the trial or study design used to generate RWE can provide adequate scientific evidence to answer or help answer the regulatory question 3. Whether the study conduct meets FDA regulatory requirements (e.g., for study monitoring and data collection)

rules.rule_text: Evidence from traditional clinical trials will not be considered RWE under FDA's RWE Program; only hybrid or pragmatic clinical trial designs and observational studies that generate evidence from sources other than traditional clinical trials may qualify as RWE.
rules.source_section: Introduction, Clinical Trials and Observational Studies Covered by the RWE Program
rules.verbatim_quote: Under FDA's RWE Program, evidence from traditional clinical trials will not be considered RWE. However, various hybrid or pragmatic trial designs and observational studies could generate RWE. FDA's RWE Program will cover clinical trials that generate RWE in some capacity (i.e., sources other than traditional clinical trials) and observational studies.

rules.rule_text: The strength of RWE submitted in support of a regulatory decision depends on the clinical study methodology, the reliability (data accrual and data quality control/data assurance), and the relevance of the underlying RWD.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Assessing Fitness of RWD, Assessing Data Reliability and Relevance
rules.verbatim_quote: The strength of RWE submitted in support of a regulatory decision depends on the clinical study methodology and the reliability (data accrual and data quality control (data assurance)) and relevance of the underlying data.

rules.rule_text: FDA does not endorse one type of RWD over another; data should be selected based on their suitability to address specific regulatory questions.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Assessing Fitness of RWD, Assessing Data Reliability and Relevance
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, FDA does not endorse one type of RWD over another. Data should be selected based on their suitability to address specific regulatory questions.

rules.rule_text: For medical claims data used as RWD, reliability assessment must include verification that ICD codes are present and in the appropriate format for use with data systems and analysis tools.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Assessing Fitness of RWD, Assessing Data Reliability and Relevance
rules.verbatim_quote: For medical claims data, this would include the assessment that the International Classification of Diseases (ICD) codes (e.g., ICD-ver.10-Clinical modification (CM)), which describe the medical diagnosis for which a claim is submitted, are present and in the appropriate format for use with the Sentinel System CDM and analysis tools.

rules.rule_text: For EHR data used as RWD, the reliability assessment must include checking laboratory data for completeness, consistency, and trends over time, including use of reporting standards such as the LOINC system.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Assessing Fitness of RWD, Assessing Data Reliability and Relevance
rules.verbatim_quote: the reliability assessment includes checking the laboratory data for completeness, consistency, and trends over time, including the use of reporting standards such as the Logical Observation Identifiers, Names, and Codes (LOINC) system.

rules.rule_text: The relevance assessment of RWD must consider whether the data are fit for purpose and include assessment of whether the data capture relevant data on exposure, outcomes, and covariates.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Assessing Fitness of RWD, Assessing Data Reliability and Relevance
rules.verbatim_quote: the relevance assessment considers whether the data are fit for purpose and include an assessment of whether the data capture relevant data on exposure, outcomes, and covariates.

rules.rule_text: Data from other countries may be used as RWD but their fitness for use in FDA regulatory decision-making could be limited by important differences in health care systems; analyses must consider differences in medical practice, health care delivery, and data reliability and relevance compared to the United States.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Assessing Fitness of RWD, Assessing Data Reliability and Relevance
rules.verbatim_quote: Data from other countries can be another valuable source of RWD, but their fitness for use in FDA regulatory decision-making could be limited by important differences in health care systems. Using data from other countries might require analyses that consider the differences in medical practice, health care delivery, and data reliability and relevance compared to the United States.

rules.rule_text: Patient registries used as RWD sources must have sufficient processes to gather follow-up information when needed, ensure data quality, and minimize missing or incomplete data to qualify for generating RWE.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Assessing Fitness of RWD, Assessing Data Reliability and Relevance
rules.verbatim_quote: Their fitness for use in generating RWE requires sufficient processes, such as those to gather follow-up information when needed, to ensure data quality, and to minimize missing or incomplete data.

rules.rule_text: The selection of appropriate RWD sources should be based on the regulatory question of interest and the data should be collected and maintained in a way that provides an appropriate level of reliability.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Assessing Fitness of RWD, Assessing Data Reliability and Relevance
rules.verbatim_quote: the selection of appropriate RWD sources should be based on the regulatory question of interest and should be collected and maintained in a way that provides an appropriate level of reliability.

rules.rule_text: When blinding of treatment is infeasible in RWD-based trials, FDA will seek to identify situations when bias resulting from lack of blinding can be controlled using outcomes that are less likely to be influenced by knowledge of treatment assignment, such as clinically objective outcomes.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Potential for Study Designs Using RWD to Support Effectiveness, Randomized Designs Using RWD
rules.verbatim_quote: When blinding of treatment is infeasible, FDA will seek to identify situations when bias resulting from lack of blinding can potentially be controlled using outcomes that are less likely to be influenced by knowledge of treatment assignment, such as clinically objective outcomes (e.g., stroke, tumor size).

rules.rule_text: For observational studies using RWD to support effectiveness, FDA will evaluate whether there is a reliable causal inference, including whether active comparators, non-inferiority designs, and sensitivity analyses are appropriate.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Potential for Study Designs, Observational Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: In considering whether data gathered through observational study designs are appropriate to generate RWE for the purpose of supporting effectiveness determinations, FDA intends to evaluate multiple questions of interest that could affect the ability to draw a reliable causal inference.

rules.rule_text: Transparency about study design and analysis before execution is critical for ensuring confidence in RWE results from observational studies; pre-specification of sensitivity analyses and statistical diagnostics is required.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Potential for Study Designs, Observational Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to study design and data considerations, transparency about study design and analysis before execution is critical for ensuring confidence in the results.

rules.rule_text: FDA will consider policies to prevent the practice of conducting numerous retrospective studies until a desired result is obtained and then submitting only favorable results as if they were from a single study with a prespecified protocol.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Potential for Study Designs, Observational Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: The potential lack of up-front transparency, especially in retrospective observational study design and conduct, coupled with the fact that retrospective analyses in electronic datasets can be conducted multiple times relatively inexpensively with varying study design elements, makes it possible to conduct numerous retrospective studies until the desired result is obtained and then submit only favorable results as if they were the result of a single study with a prespecified protocol. FDA will consider policies to prevent such practices.

rules.rule_text: Electronic source data used in clinical investigations must comply with applicable FDA guidance on electronic source data and EHRs in clinical investigations to maintain integrity of FDA-regulated investigations.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Regulatory Considerations for Study Designs Using RWD, Use of Electronic Source Data
rules.verbatim_quote: In September 2013, FDA published the guidance Electronic Source Data in Clinical Investigations, which promotes capturing source data in electronic format and provides recommendations on the capture, review, and retention of electronic source data in FDA-regulated clinical investigations. In July 2018, FDA published the final guidance Use of Electronic Health Records in Clinical Investigations. This guidance provides recommendations on ensuring the integrity of EHR data that are collected and used as electronic source data in clinical investigations of medical products.

rules.rule_text: FDA's Part 11 regulations (21 CFR Part 11) apply to the quality, authenticity, and reliability of electronic records used in clinical investigations from their point of creation through modification, maintenance, archiving, retrieval, or transmission.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Regulatory Considerations, Use of Electronic Source Data
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA's Part 11 regulations (21 CFR part 11) focus on the quality, authenticity, and reliability of electronic records from their point of creation to their modification, maintenance, archiving, retrieval, or transmission.

rules.rule_text: For clinical studies generating RWE, FDA will examine the use of risk-based and central monitoring for clinical trials integrated into the health care system, and will consider whether currently available guidance adequately addresses inspection-related concerns for different RWD sources.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Regulatory Considerations for Clinical Studies Generating RWE
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA will also examine how FDA's regulatory requirements are applied to data from randomized clinical trials that are integrated into the health care system and observational studies when they are intended to generate RWE for regulatory decision-making. For example, we will examine the use of risk-based and central monitoring for clinical trials that are integrated into the health care system.
rules.exceptions: Other regulatory considerations outside of FDA's authority (e.g., HIPAA) will not be addressed within the FDA framework

rules.rule_text: Data standards must be assessed and implemented to ensure RWD/RWE solutions are an integral part of the full drug development and regulatory life cycle at FDA; appropriate data standards are necessary to efficiently process RWD and submit it for FDA evaluation.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Data Standards
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA will assess the data standards and implementation strategies required to use RWD/RWE at FDA, identify any gaps between those requirements and existing FDA systems, and recommend a path forward to ensure that RWD/RWE solutions are an integral part of the full drug development and regulatory life cycle at FDA.

rules.rule_text: To work with RWD across multiple sources, data may need to be put into a common format (Common Data Model) with common representation including terminologies, vocabularies, and coding schemes.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Using Trials or Studies with RWD/RWE for Effectiveness Decisions
rules.verbatim_quote: To work with RWD across multiple sources, data may need to be put into a common format, sometimes referred to as a common data model (CDM), with common representation (terminologies, vocabularies, coding schemes).

rules.rule_text: Observational studies using pharmacoepidemiology must be primarily focused on studies designed to test prespecified hypotheses; investigators should submit protocols to FDA before study initiation and final reports upon completion.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Potential for Study Designs, Observational Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: This Guidance is primarily focused on studies designed to test prespecified hypotheses, as opposed to hypothesis-generating studies, and emphasizes that investigators should submit protocols to FDA before study initiation and final reports upon completion.
cross_references: Best Practices for Conducting and Reporting Pharmacoepidemiologic Safety Studies Using Electronic Healthcare Data

rules.rule_text: External controls using RWD have limitations including difficulty in reliably selecting a comparable population and lack of standardized diagnostic criteria or equivalent outcome measures; propensity scoring and collection of RWD on current patients may improve the quality of external control data.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Potential for Study Designs, Non-randomized Single Arm Trials with External RWD Control
rules.verbatim_quote: Using external controls has limitations, including difficulties in reliably selecting a comparable population because of potential changes in medical practice, lack of standardized diagnostic criteria or equivalent outcome measures, and variability in follow-up procedures. Collection of RWD on patients currently receiving other treatments, together with statistical methods, such as propensity scoring, could improve the quality of the external control data that are used when randomization may not be feasible or ethical, provided there is adequate detail to capture relevant covariates.
cross_references: E10 Choice of Control Group and Related Issues in Clinical Trials

rules.rule_text: FDA will use an RWE Subcommittee as a cross-cutting forum that provides advisory recommendations on whether underlying data, methods, and study design elements are appropriate to provide support for a regulatory decision involving RWE.
rules.source_section: Stakeholder Engagement, Internal Engagement
rules.verbatim_quote: The RWE Subcommittee will serve as a cross-cutting forum for RWE issues and will focus on CDER's evaluation of RWE and guide policy development. Staff from the Office of New Drugs may consult with the RWE Subcommittee when evaluating the use of RWD and RWE to inform regulatory decisions about product effectiveness. The RWE Subcommittee will provide advisory recommendations as needed on whether underlying data, methods, and other study design elements are appropriate to provide support for a regulatory decision.

rules.rule_text: RWE submitted to support a supplemental application to add or modify a drug product indication, change dose/dose regimen/route of administration, add a new population, or add comparative effectiveness or safety information must satisfy the three-part FDA RWE framework evaluation.
rules.source_section: Framework for Evaluating RWD/RWE, Using Trials or Studies with RWD/RWE for Effectiveness Decisions
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA's RWE Program will evaluate the potential use of RWE to support changes to labeling about drug product effectiveness, including adding or modifying an indication, such as a change in dose, dose regimen, or route of administration; adding a new population; or adding comparative effectiveness or safety information.
